mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 5% signals tight labor market conditions that drive inflationary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in periods of UNRATE < 5% exceed 1.5% in 60% of cases or show positive Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and declining housing starts (HOUST) signals mixed supply-side dynamics where manufacturing strength offsets weak demand, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in INDPRO/HOUST co-movement scenarios show positive Rank IC > 0.05 or exceed 2% in 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real sales (RSAFS) above $340 billion combined with GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% signals robust demand conditions that drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in RSAFS/GDPC1 co-expansion scenarios fail to exceed 1.2% in 50% of cases or show negative Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2% during a Fed funds rate cut cycle signals stagflation risks, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in CPI/FEDFUNDS co-occurrence scenarios show positive Rank IC > 0.05 or exceed 1.5% in 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150k combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals labor market instability, creating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in PAYEMS/UNRATE co-occurrence scenarios show positive Rank IC > 0.05 or exceed 1% in 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction (GDPC1 < 0%) during rising housing starts (HOUST > 1800) signals weak demand amid construction booms, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in GDPC1/HOUST co-occurrence scenarios show positive Rank IC > 0.05 or exceed 2% in 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 115 combined with rising CPI (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) signals stagflatory supply shocks, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO/CPIAUCSL co-occurrence scenarios show positive Rank IC > 0.05 or exceed 1.5% in 60% of cases.
